title: Game Queries — Comprehensions
description: Use list comprehensions and generator expressions to query your game data. Create these functions: get_weapons(items) returns a list of items where type is 'weapon', get_rooms_with_items(room_items) returns a list of room names that have at least one item, get_status_display(player_state) returns a list of formatted strings for each stat, and get_item_names(inventory) returns a list of just the item names from the inventory.
starter_code:
# Text Adventure — Step 7: Game Queries with Comprehensions
# Use list comprehensions to filter and transform game data.

rooms = {
    'entrance': 'You stand at the entrance of a dark cave.',
    'hallway': 'A long hallway with torches on the walls.',
    'treasure_room': 'A glittering room full of gold and jewels.',
    'armory': 'Weapons line the walls of this dusty room.',
}

game_items = [
    {'name': 'iron_sword', 'type': 'weapon', 'value': 10},
    {'name': 'health_potion', 'type': 'potion', 'value': 25},
    {'name': 'rusty_key', 'type': 'key', 'value': 'treasure_room'},
    {'name': 'wooden_shield', 'type': 'weapon', 'value': 5},
    {'name': 'fire_staff', 'type': 'weapon', 'value': 15},
    {'name': 'mega_potion', 'type': 'potion', 'value': 50},
]

room_items = {
    'entrance': [game_items[0]],
    'hallway': [game_items[1], game_items[3]],
    'treasure_room': [],
    'armory': [game_items[4], game_items[2]],
}

player_state = {
    'name': 'Hero',
    'health': 85,
    'max_health': 100,
    'attack': 15,
    'defense': 5,
    'room': 'entrance',
    'inventory': [game_items[5]],
}

def get_weapons(items):
    """Return a list of items where type is 'weapon'. Use a list comprehension."""
    # Your code here — one line with a list comprehension
    pass

def get_rooms_with_items(room_items):
    """Return a list of room names that have at least one item.
    Use a list comprehension."""

    pass

def get_status_display(player_state):
    """Return a list of formatted status strings.
    e.g. ['Name: Hero', 'Health: 85/100', 'Attack: 15', 'Defense: 5', 'Room: entrance']
    Use a list comprehension or build the list."""
    # Your code here
    pass

def get_item_names(inventory):
    """Return a list of just the 'name' values from inventory item dicts.
    Use a list comprehension."""
    # Your code here
    pass

# Test
print("Weapons:", [w['name'] for w in get_weapons(game_items)])
print("Rooms with items:", get_rooms_with_items(room_items))
print("Status:")
for line in get_status_display(player_state):
    print(f"  {line}")
print("Item names:", get_item_names(player_state['inventory']))
test_code:
assert callable(get_weapons), 'get_weapons should be a function'
assert callable(get_rooms_with_items), 'get_rooms_with_items should be a function'
assert callable(get_status_display), 'get_status_display should be a function'
assert callable(get_item_names), 'get_item_names should be a function'
test_items = [
    {'name': 'sword', 'type': 'weapon', 'value': 10},
    {'name': 'potion', 'type': 'potion', 'value': 25},
    {'name': 'axe', 'type': 'weapon', 'value': 12},
    {'name': 'key', 'type': 'key', 'value': 'door'},
]
weapons = get_weapons(test_items)
assert isinstance(weapons, list), 'get_weapons should return a list'
assert len(weapons) == 2, f'Should find 2 weapons, got {len(weapons)}'
assert all(w['type'] == 'weapon' for w in weapons), 'All returned items should be weapons'
test_room_items = {'room_a': [{'name': 'x'}], 'room_b': [], 'room_c': [{'name': 'y'}, {'name': 'z'}]}
rooms_with = get_rooms_with_items(test_room_items)
assert isinstance(rooms_with, list), 'get_rooms_with_items should return a list'
assert sorted(rooms_with) == ['room_a', 'room_c'], f'Should find room_a and room_c, got {rooms_with}'
test_ps = {'name': 'T', 'health': 50, 'max_health': 100, 'attack': 10, 'defense': 3, 'room': 'start', 'inventory': []}
status = get_status_display(test_ps)
assert isinstance(status, list), 'get_status_display should return a list'
assert len(status) >= 3, f'Status should have at least 3 lines, got {len(status)}'
status_joined = ' '.join(status).lower()
assert '50' in status_joined, 'Status should include health value'
assert 't' in status_joined or 'T' in ' '.join(status), 'Status should include player name'
test_inv = [{'name': 'sword', 'type': 'weapon', 'value': 5}, {'name': 'gem', 'type': 'key', 'value': 'x'}]
names = get_item_names(test_inv)
assert isinstance(names, list), 'get_item_names should return a list'
assert names == ['sword', 'gem'], f"get_item_names should return ['sword', 'gem'], got {names}"
assert get_item_names([]) == [], 'Empty inventory should return empty list'
acceptance_criteria: get_weapons() returns only items with type 'weapon'
get_rooms_with_items() returns room names that have items
get_status_display() returns formatted player status strings
get_item_names() extracts just names from item dicts
hints: Weapon filter: [item for item in items if item['type'] == 'weapon']
Rooms with items: [room for room, items in room_items.items() if items]
Item names: [item['name'] for item in inventory]
An empty list is falsy in Python, so 'if items' checks for non-empty
